title: Stocks Edge Higher as Chipmakers Rebound
excerpt: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX ) (SPY ) today is up +0.46%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI ) (DIA ) is down -0.14%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX ) (QQQ...
content_html:
Rich Asplund

Mon, July 20, 2026 at 9:12 AM CDT 6 min read

The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) today is up +0.46%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) is down -0.14%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) is up +0.86%.  September E-mini S&P futures (ESU26) are up +0.43%, and September E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQU26) are up +0.78%. 

Stock indexes are mostly higher today amid a rebound in chipmakers. Short covering emerged in chipmakers and AI-infrastructure stocks ahead of earnings results of megacap technology stocks this week, beginning with Alphabet on Wednesday.   Stocks also found support after crude oil prices fell from their highs on signs that diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran war were ongoing.  Weakness in software stocks today is limiting the overall market's upside.

More News from Barchart

  • Alibaba Stock Just Got Apple's Biggest AI Endorsement. This Could Be a Game Changer for BABA.

  • Elon Musk Says If SpaceX Accomplishes Its Goals, 'It Will Be Worth More Than The Rest of Earth' — Though He's Also Said 'Money Will Stop Being Relevant'

  • AI Bubble Fears, Earnings and Other Can't Miss Items this Week

  • Stop Missing Market Moves: Get the FREE Barchart Brief – your midday dose of stock movers, trending sectors, and actionable trade ideas, delivered right to your inbox. Sign Up Now!

Geopolitical risks continue, as the US conducted a ninth straight day of airstrikes on Iran in an attempt to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, bombing military targets and communications networks.  Iran retaliated by launching drones and missiles at US bases in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Iraq and attacking tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz.  The New York Times reported today that the US is sending more warplanes to the Middle East, including F-35 and F-16 fighter jets, a possible sign that US military operations could expand in the coming days.

Also, signs that the conflict in the Middle East is widening are supporting crude oil after Houthi rebels said they will impose a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia in retaliation for what they say is the kingdom's siege on the Yemeni capital, a potential threat to Saudi Arabian crude exports through the Red Sea.

However, WTI crude oil (CLQ26) prices fell from their highs after Iran said it wouldn't abandon diplomacy and that Qatar and Pakistan had reached out to mediate an end to the conflict, proposing a 10-day cessation of strikes between the US and Iran.  Crude prices initially climbed to a 5-week high today as the resumption of hostilities between the US and Iran has reduced traffic through the Strait of Hormuz to a near standstill, tightening global oil supplies.

Geopolitical risks continue, as the US conducted a ninth straight day of airstrikes on Iran in an attempt to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, bombing military targets and communications networks. Iran retaliated by launching drones and missiles at US bases in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Iraq and attacking tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz. The New York Times reported today that the US is sending more warplanes to the Middle East, including F-35 and F-16 fighter jets, a possible sign that US military operations could expand in the coming days. Also, signs that the conflict in the Middle East is widening are supporting crude oil after Houthi rebels said they will impose a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia in retaliation for what they say is the kingdom's siege on the Yemeni capital, a potential threat to Saudi Arabian crude exports through the Red Sea. However, WTI crude oil (CLQ26) prices fell from their highs after Iran said it wouldn't abandon diplomacy and that Qatar and Pakistan had reached out to mediate an end to the conflict, proposing a 10-day cessation of strikes between the US and Iran. Crude prices initially climbed to a 5-week high today as the resumption of hostilities between the US and Iran has reduced traffic through the Strait of Hormuz to a near standstill, tightening global oil supplies.

sentences.zh: 此外，有迹象表明中东冲突正在扩大，胡塞叛乱分子表示，他们将对沙特阿拉伯实施海上封锁，以报复他们所说的沙特对也门首都的围困，这对沙特阿拉伯通过红海的原油出口构成潜在威胁。

sentences.zh: 然而，在伊朗表示不会放弃外交、卡塔尔和巴基斯坦已出手调解结束冲突、提议美国和伊朗之间停止罢工10天后，WTI原油(CLQ26)价格从高位回落。

sentences.zh: 由于美国和伊朗之间的敌对行动恢复，霍尔木兹海峡的交通几乎陷入停滞，全球石油供应紧张，原油价格今天最初攀升至五周高点。

content_html_zh:
里奇·阿斯普伦德

2026 年 7 月 20 日星期一上午 9:12 CDT 阅读 6 分钟

标准普尔 500 指数 ($SPX) (SPY) 今天上涨 +0.46%，道琼斯工业平均指数 ($DOWI) (DIA) 下跌 -0.14%，纳斯达克 100 指数 ($IUXX) (QQQ) 上涨 +0.86%。 9 月 E-mini 标准普尔期货 (ESU26) 上涨 +0.43%，9 月 E-mini 纳斯达克期货 (NQU26) 上涨 +0.78%。

由于芯片制造商的反弹，今天股指大多走高。 本周大型科技股公布财报之前，芯片制造商和人工智能基础设施股出现了空头回补，周三的 Alphabet 就是其中之一。 由于有迹象表明结束美伊战争的外交努力正在进行，原油价格从高位回落，股市也获得支撑。 今天软件股的疲软限制了整体市场的上涨。

来自条形图的更多新闻

  • 阿里巴巴股票刚刚获得苹果公司最大的人工智能认可。 这可能会改变阿里巴巴的游戏规则。
  • 埃隆·马斯克表示，如果 SpaceX 实现其目标，“它将比地球其他地方更有价值”——尽管他也表示“金钱将不再重要”
  • 本周人工智能泡沫恐惧、收益和其他不容错过的内容
  • 不再错过市场走势：获取免费的条形图简介 - 您中午的股票走势、趋势板块和可行的交易想法，将直接发送到您的收件箱。 立即注册！

地缘政治风险持续存在，美国连续第九天对伊朗进行空袭，试图重新开放霍尔木兹海峡，轰炸军事目标和通信网络。 作为报复，伊朗向科威特、约旦、巴林和伊拉克的美国基地发射了无人机和导弹，并袭击了经过霍尔木兹海峡的油轮。 《纽约时报》今天报道称，美国正在向中东派遣更多战机，包括 F-35 和 F-16 战斗机，这可能表明美国军事行动可能在未来几天扩大。

此外，有迹象表明中东冲突正在扩大，胡塞叛乱分子表示，他们将对沙特阿拉伯实施海上封锁，以报复他们所说的沙特对也门首都的围困，这对沙特阿拉伯通过红海的原油出口构成潜在威胁。

然而，在伊朗表示不会放弃外交、卡塔尔和巴基斯坦已出手调解结束冲突、提议美国和伊朗之间停止罢工10天后，WTI原油(CLQ26)价格从高位回落。 由于美国和伊朗之间的敌对行动恢复，霍尔木兹海峡的交通几乎陷入停滞，全球石油供应紧张，原油价格今天最初攀升至五周高点。
